name: Baked Chicken Kiev
recipeIngredient: 4 Chicken breasts, 1 lb each, skin-boned and split
4 oz Monterey jack cheese, sliced into 8 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken and pat dry.
2. Place one piece at a time, skin side down, between two sheets of plastic wrap, and pound with a flat surface mallet until each breast is about 1/8 inch thick.
3. In a shallow bowl, combine bread crumbs, parmesan cheese, garlic salt, pepper, and 1 tsp of the oregano. Set aside.
4. In a small bowl, stir together 1/4 cup butter, remaining oregano, and parsley.
5. Arrange the pounded chicken breasts skin side down.
6. Spread about 1/2 tablespoon of the butter mixture across each piece, about an inch from one of the long sides, and place a strip of the Jack cheese over the butter.
7. Fold the short ends over the filling, then fold in the long sides and roll to enclose the filling.
8. Dip each bundle into the 6 tbsp of melted butter, drain, and roll in the bread crumb mixture until coated evenly and well.
9. Place the bundles, seam side down and slightly separated, into a 10x15 inch rimmed baking pan.
10. Drizzle with the remaining butter.
11. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
12. Bake uncovered in a 425°F oven until chicken is no longer pink when slightly slashed (do not cut the filling!), about 20 minutes. Serve immediately.
MEXICAN STYLE KIEV:
Follow the directions above, but omit the oregano. Instead use a mixture of 1 tsp chili powder and 1/2 tsp ground cumin. Using a can of whole green chiles, discard the pit and any seeds. Cut the chilies into 1 inch strips and divide into eight equal portions. When filling the chicken breasts, place one portion of the chile atop each strip of cheese. Serve the chicken with the following tomato sauce: Stir together 1-15 oz can of tomato sauce with 1/2 tsp each ground cumin and sugar and 1/4 cup sliced green onions (including tops). Cook over medium heat, stirring until hot. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and hot pepper sauce.
